items.title: The Iran war has strengthened Ukraine in surprising ways. Could a ceasefire with Russia be closer?
items.summary: President Zelensky has been visiting the Gulf to demonstrate his country's military nous.

items.title: Three dead in suspected virus outbreak on Atlantic cruise ship
items.summary: The World Health Organization said one case of hantavirus infection had been confirmed, with five more suspected cases under investigation.

items.title: Russian strikes kill 10 as Zelensky says Ukraine hits oil tankers and terminal
items.summary: Fatalities have been reported around Ukraine as Kyiv says Russian "shadow fleet" tankers were hit by drones.

items.title: Nigeria plans to repatriate nationals willing to leave South Africa after attacks
items.summary: There has been a wave of anti-migrant protests in South Africa, some of which have turned violent.

items.title: Police officer lowered into crocodile-infested river to recover human remains
items.summary: Police suspect the human remains belong to a businessman swept away by floodwater last week.

items.title: The clandestine network smuggling Starlink tech into Iran to beat internet blackout
items.summary: Sahand tells the BBC World Service he sends satellite internet terminals into Iran to help show "the real picture".

items.title: Germany troop cuts send wrong signal to Russia, say two top US Republicans
items.summary: The chairs of the House and Senate armed services committees said withdrawing 5,000 service personnel risked undermining deterrence.

items.title: Concern for jailed Iranian Nobel laureate as brother fears she is dying
items.summary: Her family said the 54-year-old had been taken from prison to a local hospital after a sharp deterioration in her health.

items.title: Two US service members reported missing in Morocco, officials say
items.summary: A search and rescue operation is underway for the service members, who are believed to have been involved in an accident, according to authorities.

items.title: Man charged with murder of 5-year-old Australian girl
items.summary: The girl's death sparked an outbreak of violence in the central Australian town from where she disappeared.

items.title: 'If we sleep, they bite': Rats and weasels infest camps for displaced Gazans
items.summary: In the Gaza Strip, the daily battles are now with rats, weasels, and other pests spreading diseases.

items.title: Thirteen killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, health ministry says
items.summary: Four women and a child are among the dead, as fighting between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah continues despite a ceasefire.

items.title: Oil tanker hijacked off coast of Yemen and taken towards Somalia
items.summary: It is the second time an oil tanker has been hijacked in the area in the past 10 days.

items.title: US Supreme Court asked to restore abortion pill access
items.summary: A lower court has limited mail-order access to the abortion pill mifepristone, which the drug's maker says will cause "irreparable harm".

items.title: Cuba condemns new US sanctions as 'illegal' and 'abusive'
items.summary: The new measures come on top of a US blockade of oil to Cuba that has caused widespread blackouts and fuel shortages.

items.title: Trump tells Congress ceasefire means he does not need their approval for Iran war
items.summary: The president writes that hostilities "have terminated" because of the ceasefire, arguing he does not need congressional authorisation.

items.title: Who shot a Secret Service officer at the Trump press dinner?
items.summary: Court papers do not explicitly accuse the suspect of shooting the officer, even as officials say it was not "friendly fire".

items.title: Trump claims other presidents flouted war powers law. It's a mixed record
items.summary: Both Bushes and Reagan won authorisation for wars, but Obama and Clinton ducked the requirement.

items.title: Ex-F1 driver and Paralympic champion Zanardi dies
items.summary: Former Formula 1 driver and Paralympic champion Alex Zanardi dies at the age of 59.

items.title: Taiwan president visits Eswatini days after blaming China for cancelled trip
items.summary: It is unclear how he reached Eswatini. China described his visit as a "stowaway-style escape farce".

items.title: War criminal Mladic close to death, say lawyers asking judge for jail release
items.summary: Mladic, 84, was jailed for life for genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity during the Bosnian war in 1992-95.

items.title: Billions of meals at risk due to Iran war, says fertiliser boss
items.summary: A shortage of fertiliser due to the Iran conflict could reduce crop yields and push prices higher, says the boss of Yara.

items.title: Missing Oscar found after Academy Award winner blocked from taking it on flight
items.summary: The airline said the Academy Award was safely in its care in Frankfurt and was arranging its return.

items.title: Turkish police arrest more than 500 people at May Day rallies
items.summary: Turkey sees significant police deployments on 1 May each year, when marches led by workers and unions are held.

items.title: Israeli police arrest man after nun attacked in Jerusalem
items.summary: The French nun was pushed over and kicked in the apparently unprovoked assault, amid a rise in harassment of Christians in the city by Jewish extremists.

items.title: Israel releases all but two activists in Greece after intercepting Gaza aid flotilla
items.summary: The Global Sumud Flotilla denounced the interception as "piracy", while Israel's foreign ministry called the flotilla a "PR stunt".

items.title: Kenya battles to stop the 'goons and guns' as fears of political violence grow
items.summary: More than 100 gangs are operating nationwide as the growing use of political thugs raises fears of election violence.

items.title: Voters will judge Trump on the economy - how is it doing?
items.summary: How much Donald Trump can do with the time he has left as president will depend massively on the cost of living.

items.title: Laufey on making jazz cool again (and the fish that brought out her inner rage)
items.summary: The Icelandic star reflects on a phenomenal year, and the music video that let her go "primal".

items.title: Dragonflies in distress: Scientists sound alarm in India's ecological hotspot
items.summary: A new study reveals a decline in dragonfly and damselfly species in India's Western Ghats.

items.title: The world wants to eliminate cervical cancer - how Australian scientists led the way
items.summary: For the first time, Australia has recorded no new cervical cancer diagnoses in women under 25. To get there, they vaccinated young men.

items.title: 'This tree was planted by my ancestor hundreds of years ago and my family settled here'
items.summary: Oral history traces a family's roots to a tree planted in the 13th Century in a Ghanaian fishing town.

items.title: Thousands of 'lost Canadians' have applied for dual citizenship - is Canada ready?
items.summary: Changes to Canadian citizenship rules have led to thousands of new applications - many coming from the US.

items.title: Huge crowd attends free Shakira Copacabana beach concert
items.summary: Shakira's appearance followed shows by Lady Gaga and Madonna, who have also performed on Copacabana beach in previous years.

items.title: How we reported in -30C temperatures in Canada's Arctic
items.summary: BBC's Nadine Yousif and Eloise Alanna spent five days travelling with the country's military rangers through frostbite and sub-zero temperatures.

items.title: New CCTV footage appears to shows when shots were fired at Washington press dinner
items.summary: The video was released after claims that a secret service member sustained injuries in an act of 'friendly fire'.

items.title: Why is China banning drone sales in Beijing?
items.summary: The BBC's Laura Bicker looks into why China has tightened regulations on drones, with authorities citing public safety concerns.
